topic.what: SS-based secure comparison achieving two-round complexity to match GC, plus reduced online bit complexity to 6n
topic.how: Uses round-absorption via multi-fan-in AND gates, new multiplication technique for trivial shares, and custom-tailored Boolean circuits for quaternary integers
topic.why_matters: Breaks longstanding barrier of SS-based comparison requiring 3+ rounds vs GC's 2; reduces online bandwidth from 8n to 6n asymptotically

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of communication rounds for online phase
characteristics.context: First SS-based comparison with 2-round complexity matching GC

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of communication rounds for online phase
characteristics.context: Second 2-round protocol trading offline for online complexity
25% reduction vs state-of-art; q ≈ n/2 digits with 6 bits each

characteristics.what_it_measures: Bits transmitted during online phase for n=64
characteristics.context: 25% reduction for 64-bit inputs with λ=128, μ=512
Tradeoff: Protocol 2 reduces offline from O(n³) to O(n²) at cost of increasing online to O(n²)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of online rounds for Protocol 3
characteristics.context: O(log n) but with base 3 vs binary

novelty: First SS-based comparison matching GC's two-round complexity
New round-absorption technique via multi-fan-in gates
IV-AND operation for multiplication involving auxiliary single-party input
Custom-tailored Boolean circuit for quaternary integer comparison with reusable factors
Correlated 3-fan-in AND operation for efficient tree computation
open_problems: Achieving one-round comparison (requires one-round equality testing or new paradigm)
Further reducing online bit complexity below 6n (may require novel encoding techniques)
